stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Oceanographers use traditional research ships to study the open ocean.
  • Research ships are expensive to operate and cannot be deployed in many locations at once.
  • Research ships are also limited to areas safely navigable by large vessels.
  • Argo floats, autonomous drifting sensors that weigh only about 60 pounds, can collect ocean data continuously and travel freely on currents.
  • Argo floats have been proposed as a complement to traditional research ships.
stem: The student wants to explain an advantage of Argo floats. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Argo floats, which weigh only about 60 pounds, can collect ocean data continuously in areas that are unsafe for the large vessels oceanographers traditionally use.
Oceanographers currently use research ships, but Argo floats have been proposed as a complement.
Though they are different sizes, both Argo floats and research ships can be used to study the ocean.
Despite their cost, traditional research ships continue to be useful for ocean study.

rationale: Choice A is the best answer because it explains a specific advantage of Argo floats—continuous data collection in places too risky for large vessels—and ties it to their light weight. Choice B is incorrect because it states that Argo floats complement ships without explaining an advantage. Choice C is incorrect because it emphasizes similarity, not advantage. Choice D is incorrect because it describes a virtue of research ships rather than Argo floats.
